mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to equity cash flows, mechanically lifting S&P 500 valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 5.2 percent, labor market deterioration triggers recession fears that compress equity risk premiums, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in more than 60 percent of triggered instances despite negative signal, or Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in industrial production and housing starts signal a synchronized contraction in real economic activity, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns do not exhibit statistically significant negative skew when both proxies decline simultaneously compared to baseline periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate crosses above 4.5 percent, the cost of capital exceeds the sustainable growth rate of corporate earnings, forcing equity multiples to contract and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in more than 50 percent of instances where FEDFUNDS stays above 4.5 percent for consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real disposable income measured by personal savings and consumption flows directly supports corporate revenue growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or forward returns show no positive correlation with RSAFS growth rates.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-inflation environment, strong employment growth fails to translate into equity gains because wage pressures compress profit margins, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are not significantly lower when PAYEMS growth exceeds 200k monthly and CPIAUCSL exceeds 245 compared to low-inflation labor strength periods.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained drop in the industrial production index below 102.0 signals supply chain bottlenecks resolving into deflationary weakness, which reduces corporate pricing power and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Forward returns do not exhibit negative skew when INDPRO remains below 102.0 for two consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid growth in real GDP combined with stagnant housing starts indicates an economy overheating in services while cooling in construction, which historically precedes equity drawdowns as the Federal Reserve tightens policy, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains above 0.05 when GDPC1 growth exceeds 3.0 percent annualized and HOUST declines quarter-over-quarter.
